channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center in secretive process
item.description: The removal of President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center after a judge's order was shrouded in secrecy. NPR's Frank Langfitt joined onlookers as workers hid the process from the public.
item.content:encoded: The removal of President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center after a judge's order was shrouded in secrecy. NPR's Frank Langfitt joined onlookers as workers hid the process from the public.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: What covering the World Series of Birding tells us about teen teamwork
item.description: Sleep-deprived teens, devoted dads, and hundreds of birds. NPR spends twenty four hours at the World Series of Birding.
item.content:encoded: Sleep-deprived teens, devoted dads, and hundreds of birds. NPR spends twenty four hours at the World Series of Birding.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: How some U.S. states are relaxing alcohol regulations for the World Cup
item.description: Several states are loosening alcohol restrictions during the World Cup. NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks with Corean Reynolds, Boston's Director of Nightlife Economy, about how her city is handling this.
item.content:encoded: Several states are loosening alcohol restrictions during the World Cup. NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks with Corean Reynolds, Boston's Director of Nightlife Economy, about how her city is handling this.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Dr. Art Ulene plans to mark his 90th birthday by climbing Tanzania's Mount Kilimanjaro
item.description: NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks to retired physician Art Ulene about his plans to celebrate his 90th birthday next month by summitting Mount Kilimanjaro, Africa's highest peak.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks to retired physician Art Ulene about his plans to celebrate his 90th birthday next month by summitting Mount Kilimanjaro, Africa's highest peak.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Trump says U.S. military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang
item.description: President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Kennedy Center removes Trump's name from the building
item.description: Workers finished removing President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.
item.content:encoded:
Workers finished removing President Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: A judge clears the way for Trump's name to be removed from the Kennedy Center
item.description: On Friday, a federal judge denied an appeal by the Kennedy Center's board of trustees for a stay on the removal of President Trump's name from the building. Then the Trump administration filed for an emergency stay, and that too was denied.
item.content:encoded: On Friday, a federal judge denied an appeal by the Kennedy Center's board of trustees for a stay on the removal of President Trump's name from the building. Then the Trump administration filed for an emergency stay, and that too was denied.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Who is Todd Blanche, President Trump's nominee to lead the Department of Justice?
item.description: President Trump formally nominated his former personal attorney, Todd Blanche, to be the next attorney general. His rise could be a window into the future of the DOJ.
item.content:encoded: President Trump formally nominated his former personal attorney, Todd Blanche, to be the next attorney general. His rise could be a window into the future of the DOJ.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Trump says he has canceled planned strikes on Iran and peace deal is near — again
item.description: President Trump has canceled planned strikes in Iran, claiming, once again, that a peace deal is near. This is just the latest salvo in a series of whiplash proclamations when it comes to the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has canceled planned strikes in Iran, claiming, once again, that a peace deal is near. This is just the latest salvo in a series of whiplash proclamations when it comes to the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: A key U.S. spy tool has lapsed — now what?
item.description: The government says more than 60% of the president's daily intelligence briefing relies on information collected under a tool known as FISA Section 702. But Congress has struggled to renew it.
item.content:encoded:
The government says more than 60% of the president's daily intelligence briefing relies on information collected under a tool known as FISA Section 702. But Congress has struggled to renew it.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel
